meaning: n.女房东,女地主,(旅馆等的)老板娘
examples: The landlady patted her hair nervously.
There was a note under the door from my landlady.
When my landlady said she wanted to keep $380 of my deposit to paint the walls, I threw a fit.
On a good day, they could each make more than two hundred after paying our landlady.
Cellini's landlady hasn't connected with the world since the 1950s--even microwave ovens utterly baffle her.
